# COVERED

> COVERED is a UK social enterprise that installs smart wall-mounted dispensers giving out free, plastic-free and biodegradable period products. The service is funded entirely by brands advertising on the dispensers' 32-inch digital screens — users pay nothing, venues host at no cost, and advertisers fund the products. Tagline: "Free Period Products, Paid for by Advertising. We got you covered." The first pilot launches in Exeter, UK.

## Key Pages

- [Home](https://coveredaccess.com/)
- [The problem — UK period poverty statistics](https://coveredaccess.com/#problem)
- [How it works — QR, ad, dispense journey](https://coveredaccess.com/#how)
- [For brands — advertising on COVERED dispensers](https://coveredaccess.com/#brands)
- [Partners](https://coveredaccess.com/#partners)
- [For venues — host a dispenser at zero cost](https://coveredaccess.com/#venues)
- [Locations — Exeter pilot and UK roadmap](https://coveredaccess.com/#locations)
- [FAQ](https://coveredaccess.com/#faq)

## Key Facts

- More than 1 in 10 (11%) UK women and people who menstruate struggled to afford period products in the last year (ActionAid UK polling).
- COVERED dispensers give out free 4-packs of plastic-free, biodegradable pads and tampons.
- The model is self-sustaining: advertising revenue funds product supply, restocking and maintenance.
- Venues host units at zero cost; COVERED handles installation, stock and maintenance.
- The initial Exeter pilot uses open (non-gated) dispensing and collects no personal data.
- The platform is being built to UK GDPR and PECR standards, privacy by design.
